description_long: This object consists of three mushrooms growing closely together on a slightly raised, pale, circular base. The largest mushroom stands centrally, featuring a broad, domed cap with a reddish-orange color and prominent white spots, reminiscent of the Amanita muscaria species. Two smaller mushrooms flank the central one, each with similarly colored caps and white speckling, but with smaller diameters and lower heights. The stems are thick, cylindrical, and brown, supporting the caps above the base. The base itself is smooth and light gray, resembling a patch of compacted earth or stone. The overall appearance is stylized and clean, with a glossy finish on the caps and a matte look on the base.
description: A cluster of three red-orange mushrooms with white spots, growing from a smooth, pale circular base. The central mushroom is the largest, flanked by two smaller ones, all with glossy caps and thick brown stems.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three mushrooms clearly, with the largest in the center and two smaller ones on either side, all caps visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the largest mushroom cap and the tops of the two smaller mushrooms, with the base partially shadowed.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, but with a slight angle showing more of the right side of the mushrooms and the base.
description_view_3: Back angled view, focusing on the rear of the largest mushroom and the tops of the smaller mushrooms, with the base's curvature visible.
